title: Dual-CV ensemble with sigmoid-calibrated blend, not raw single-model probabilities
summary: For AUC tasks, diversity across model families often beats extra feature tinkering.
rationale: For AUC tasks, diversity across model families often beats extra feature tinkering. A grouped-fold CatBoost model, a histogram GBDT model, and a simpler linear/hazard model can be blended; sigmoid calibration preserves ranking, unlike isotonic which may introduce ties.
implementation_hint: Generate out-of-fold predictions from 2-3 diverse models under `GroupKFold(Race_Year)` and a harsher holdout such as leave-one-year-out. Blend by rank-average or logistic stacking on OOF predictions, then optionally apply sigmoid calibration on the stack using disjoint folds.
expected_effect: Usually improves robustness and public LB transfer even if each single model is only slightly different; especially useful if local CV is optimistic.
risk: Calibration done on the same folds as model fitting is leakage. Isotonic may slightly hurt AUC by creating ties.
